questions.text: How do modern define-by-run (dynamic graph) autograd engines trace the operations required for backpropagation?
options: By dynamically recording operations on a tape-like execution graph during the forward pass, and traversing it in reverse during the backward pass.
By compiling a static symbolic representation of the entire network architecture before training data is fed.
By calculating numerical gradients using finite difference perturbations for every variable on each optimization step.
By generating a lookup table of analytic derivatives for all possible tensor shapes during framework installation.
questions.correct_answer: By dynamically recording operations on a tape-like execution graph during the forward pass, and traversing it in reverse during the backward pass.
questions.explanation: Dynamic graph frameworks like PyTorch use tape-based automatic differentiation. During the forward pass, operations are executed eagerly, and any operation involving tensors that require gradients is recorded on a dynamic execution DAG. The backward pass then traverses this DAG in reverse topological order.

questions.text: When writing a custom autograd function mapping inputs X to outputs Y, what must the backward pass compute to propagate gradients correctly?
options: The Vector-Jacobian Product (VJP) of the incoming output gradients and the Jacobian matrix of the operation.
The full, explicit Jacobian matrix of shape output_dim x input_dim.
The element-wise derivative of the inputs with respect to the output layer weights.
The numerical gradient computed via two-sided finite difference approximations.
questions.correct_answer: The Vector-Jacobian Product (VJP) of the incoming output gradients and the Jacobian matrix of the operation.
questions.explanation: The backward method of a custom autograd function receives the incoming gradient vector of the loss with respect to the outputs, and must return the Vector-Jacobian Product (VJP) to calculate the gradient of the loss with respect to the inputs without building the full Jacobian matrix in memory.
